title: PennyLane Device
description: Logical description of a PennyLane device — the executable target for a quantum circuit. PennyLane devices abstract simulators (default.qubit, lightning.qubit, lightning.gpu, default.mixed) and hardware backends (IBM Q, IonQ, Rigetti, AWS Braket, Strawberry Fields) behind a single interface.

plugin.description: PennyLane plugin providing the device, e.g. 'pennylane-lightning', 'pennylane-qiskit'.

wires.description: Number of qubits or photonic modes exposed by the device.

shots.description: Number of measurement shots. Null indicates analytic (exact-expectation) execution where supported.

backend.description: Underlying backend identifier (for plugins that wrap multiple hardware/simulator targets).

supportsGradient.description: Whether the device supports a native (e.g. parameter-shift, adjoint, backprop) gradient method.

diffMethods.description: List of differentiation methods supported by the device.

capabilities.description: Free-form capability map returned by the device (model, returns_state, returns_probs, supports_finite_shots, etc.).
